metadata.title:
SLEPT BENEATH


THE BRIDGE

BUT PickPocKET SOUGHT
HIM EVEN THERE AND LEFT
NOTHING ON HIM BUT HIS
JAG.

pages.raw_text:
Reports of pocket picking are
multiplying at the police station as
the registration for Indian land is
drawing to close. Even bed
beneath the Monroe street bridge
afforded William Lund no exemp-
ton from the operations OF the vel
vety fingered crooks las, night.


William had about SIS, ticket to
st. Maries, Idaho. and good sup
ply of Supplying liquids aboard
when he lay down under the high
bridge to forget dull cares. He
slumbersd peacefully only an hour
or so, but that was too much for
him to sleep outside. of safety
vault. The thieves had actually
cut the pockets out of his trousers
and everything was gone except
the holes.


Thomas K Bailey of Sedalfa,
Mo-, who came to register for a
claim, was relieved OF a iNGv York
draft on the Third National bank
of Sedalia for $50. other papers
valued at about SIS and his ticket
back home. This happened at the
terminal depot, seemingly the ia
vorfte stamping grounds of the
dips"


Glen Perkins, rooming at the
Brown hotel, is bemoaning the loss
of his elegant gold watch. He says
1t was slipped out OF his pocket by
pickpockets while on crowded
train from Missoula. Numerous
other reports of this same nature
are being investigated by the plain
clothes men in the police depart
ment. Many of the suspicious char
acters have been ordered out of
the city and others have been
locked up for vagrancy, out the au-
thorities are unable to catch any
Of the dips" at work.
